text.title: systemd User Services Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the systemd User Services methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: User systemd services REQUIRE `loginctl enable-linger <user>`. Without it, units die at logout.
rule.rationale: systemd --user is bound to active sessions by default; linger is the explicit always-on flag.

rule.statement: Long-running units use Restart=on-failure (NOT Restart=always). RestartSec=5s. StartLimitBurst=5 StartLimitIntervalSec=60.
rule.rationale: Restart=always loops on intentional `systemctl stop`; on-failure respects the operator's intent. Burst+interval prevents thrash storms.

rule.statement: Secrets load via EnvironmentFile=/etc/<svc>.env (chmod 600). NEVER `Environment=SECRET=...` inline.
rule.rationale: Inline values leak through `systemctl show`; EnvironmentFile is gated by file permissions.

rule.statement: UnitPlan names a single owner. 'team' / 'we' / '' are forbidden.
StandardOutput=journal + StandardError=journal. NEVER pipe to a log file managed by the app.
rule.rationale: journalctl + log retention is the system's job; app-managed log files miss rotation + need cleanup cron.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-systemd-user-services.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
